zotero.abstractNote: Variational autoencoders (VAEs) are typically trained by maximizing the evidence lower bound (ELBO) on the log-marginal likelihood of the data. The ELBO is derived using a simple prior, such as a standard Gaussian, which is often too restrictive to capture the true structure of the data. We propose a method for learning more flexible priors by using a resampling procedure. Our approach, which we call the resampled prior, allows for a wide range of complex, multi-modal prior distributions while remaining computationally efficient. We demonstrate that our method leads to improved generative performance on several standard benchmarks.
zotero.conferenceName: 22nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 22nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
